report_name: Automated 'Megalodon' Attack Compromises Over 5,500 GitHub Repos by Injecting Malicious CI/CD Workflows

threat_actors.name: TeamPCP
threat_actors.description: A threat group whose methodology is noted as being similar to the Megalodon campaign, indicating a broader trend of financially motivated actors targeting developers through supply chain attacks.

attack_patterns.name: Valid Accounts
attack_patterns.description: The Megalodon campaign leveraged legitimate, stolen developer GitHub credentials to authenticate and push malicious commits to thousands of repositories.

attack_patterns.name: Supply Chain Compromise
attack_patterns.description: The attack compromised the software supply chain by injecting malicious code into the CI/CD pipelines of over 5,500 GitHub repositories, backdooring their build processes.

attack_patterns.name: Compromise Software Dependencies and Development Tools
attack_patterns.description: Attackers modified CI/CD workflow files, such as GitHub Actions, compromising the development toolchain to inject malicious steps into the automated build and deployment process.

attack_patterns.name: Unsecured Credentials
attack_patterns.description: The malicious CI/CD workflows were designed to steal secrets from the build environment, such as GITHUB_TOKEN and AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID, which are often stored as environment variables.

attack_patterns.name: Command and Scripting Interpreter: Unix Shell
attack_patterns.description: The malicious steps injected into CI/CD workflows likely used Unix shell commands, such as curl, to exfiltrate stolen secrets from the build environment.

attack_patterns.name: Compile After Delivery
attack_patterns.description: The malicious code injected into the CI/CD workflow is not executed until the pipeline runs, effectively compiling or interpreting the payload after delivery to the repository.

attack_patterns.name: Exfiltration Over C2 Channel
attack_patterns.description: The malicious workflow step exfiltrated stolen secrets (environment variables) from the CI/CD environment to an attacker-controlled server, likely using a curl or wget command.

malware.name: Infostealer Malware
malware.description: Used to harvest GitHub developer credentials from compromised machines, which were then used as the initial access vector for the Megalodon supply chain attack.

tools.name: curl
tools.description: The malicious CI/CD workflow step likely used a curl command to send all environment variables from the build environment to the attacker's exfiltration server.

campaigns.name: Megalodon
campaigns.description: A large-scale, automated supply chain attack campaign that compromised over 5,500 public GitHub repositories by pushing malicious commits that altered CI/CD workflow files. The campaign leveraged stolen developer credentials harvested by infostealer malware.

identities.name: GitHub
identities.description: The code hosting platform where over 5,500 public repositories were compromised by the Megalodon campaign.

identities.name: npm
identities.description: The package registry that took defensive measures, such as invalidating access tokens, in response to the rising threat of token theft demonstrated by attacks like Megalodon.

identities.name: Public GitHub Repositories
identities.description: A class of over 5,500 public repositories on GitHub whose CI/CD workflows were compromised by the Megalodon campaign.

infrastructure.name: Attacker-controlled Exfiltration Server
infrastructure.description: A server controlled by the Megalodon campaign attackers to receive exfiltrated secrets, such as API keys and tokens, from compromised CI/CD build environments.

indicators.name: Suspicious Commit to GitHub Workflow File
indicators.pattern:
title: Suspicious Modification of GitHub Workflow File
id: 2b9c1a8e-5d7f-4b1a-9c8d-3e4f5a6b7c8d
status: experimental
description: Detects modification of GitHub Actions workflow files (.github/workflows/*.yml), which could indicate a CI/CD poisoning attempt like the Megalodon campaign.
references:
  - https://thehackernews.com/2026/05/megalodon-github-attack-targets-5561.html
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-05-24
tags:
  - attack.t1195.001
level: high
logsource:
  category: file_event
  product: linux
detection:
  selection:
    TargetFilename|contains: '/.github/workflows/'
    TargetFilename|endswith: '.yml'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects modifications to GitHub Actions workflow files, a key TTP of the Megalodon campaign.

indicators.name: Secret Exfiltration via cURL in CI/CD Pipeline
indicators.pattern:
title: Potential Secret Exfiltration via cURL in CI/CD Pipeline
id: 8e2d2b17-d44c-4bf9-38f9-8ee46b3cd3f8
status: experimental
description: Detects the use of 'curl' to potentially exfiltrate environment variables, a technique used in CI/CD supply chain attacks like Megalodon to steal secrets.
references:
  - https://thehackernews.com/2026/05/megalodon-github-attack-targets-5561.html
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-05-24
tags:
  - attack.t1041
  - attack.t1059.004
level: high
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: linux
detection:
  selection:
    Image|endswith: '/curl'
    CommandLine|contains:
      - ' -d '
      - ' --data '
      - ' -X POST '
  selection_url:
    CommandLine|contains:
      - 'http://'
      - 'https://'
  filter_github:
    CommandLine|contains: 'api.github.com'
  condition: selection and selection_url and not filter_github
indicators.description: Detects the use of cURL within a CI/CD pipeline to exfiltrate data, a method used by the Megalodon campaign to steal secrets.
